source: Incus Server - 10 Automating Snapshots: A refresh script and cron on the primary server

questions.q: What does chapter 10 automate (versus the built-in scheduler in chapter 9)?
options: The copy/refresh of containers over to the snapshot server (a script run by cron on incus-primary)
The creation of the ZFS pool
questions.why: Chapter 9 schedules snapshots per container; chapter 10 automates pushing/refreshing them to the snapshot server.

questions.q: Which command refreshes a single container onto the snapshot server?
questions.why: copy --refresh updates the copy on incus-snapshot in place.

questions.q: How does the refresh script iterate over every container?
options: It loops over the output of `lxc ls -c n --format csv` (the container names)
questions.why: A for loop over `lxc ls -c n --format csv` refreshes each named container.

questions.q: Where is the automation scheduled, and why log its output to a file?
options: In the incusadmin user's crontab, redirecting output to a log so you know whether the refresh worked
questions.why: The incusadmin crontab runs the script (e.g. 22:00) and writes a refreshlog -- important to confirm success.

questions.q: The automated refresh sometimes fails for one container. What do you do?
questions.why: A single failed container can be refreshed by hand with the same copy --refresh command.
